entity_mapping_note: Scanner slug johnson-amp-johnson maps to fortune-500.json 'Johnson & Johnson' (rank 317, composite 27.5) — NOT 'Johnson Controls' (rank 87, composite 50). Baseline corrected to 27.5.
key_evidence: FRAMING CORRECTION: $10.5B talc settlement APPROVED by Judge Kaplan (D.N.J.) Feb 14, 2026 — prior scan framing of 'bankruptcy escape denied' was factually wrong
Claimants voted 83% in favor; fund distributed over 25 years to ovarian-cancer and mesothelioma claimants; 62,847+ registered claimants; claims accepted from Mar 1
Settlement carries NO admission of wrongdoing or liability — caps accountability credit (AB1/AB3 limiter)
Third-attempt 'Texas two-step' bankruptcy route criticized as accountability-avoidance via legal procedure (Consumer Notice)
Reparative element: large-scale court-approved compensation mechanism reaching a harmed class (AB5/EQU5 partial credit)

events.label: Court-approved $10.5B talc settlement (reparative) without admission of liability (accountability-capped)

methodology_note: SETTLEMENT-WITHOUT-ADMISSION methodology question resolved as two-sided: ACC 1.4->1.6 (partial credit for delivering court-approved compensation mechanism, capped by no-admission structure) and EQU 2.0->2.1 (compensation flows to harmed cancer-claimant class). Framing correction of prior-scan error is the primary action; net composite +0.9 sub-5pt. Remains Developing. Reconstruction of published dims = 27.5 (exact). Confidence MEDIUM given no-admission structure and disputed per-claimant adequacy.

framing_correction.factual_status: WRONG — settlement approved Feb 14, 2026 ($10.5B / 25 years / 83% claimant approval)
framing_correction.action: Correct evidence base; J&J succeeded in channeling claims through court-approved settlement.
